blurb: The Middle Eastern staple - crispy chickpea fritters with tahini sauce, hummus, fresh herbs.

prov.popular_in: Levantine countries and Middle Eastern restaurants globally
prov.famous_for: Crispy spiced chickpea fritters served with tahini and fresh vegetables
prov.consists_of: Dried chickpeas ground with herbs and spices, formed into balls, deep-fried until golden
prov.name_origin: From Arabic 'falafil' - peppers; possibly invented in Egypt as meat substitute during Lent
prov.why_loved: The crispy exterior, fluffy interior, and bright herb-spice flavour make them irresistible
prov.method_detail: Stovetop - dried chickpeas soaked 12-24 hours, ground with parsley, coriander, garlic, spices, formed, fried 3-4 min at 175°C

brief.lead: Authentic falafel begins the night before with dried chickpeas - never canned. Soaked chickpeas are ground fresh with herbs and spices, shaped, and deep-fried until shatteringly crisp outside and fluffy inside. Serve with tahini sauce, warm pita, and a bright herb salad for the complete Middle Eastern plate experience.
expect: Soak
Dried chickpeas need 12-24 hours hydration - this is non-negotiable for texture
Prep
Ground chickpea mixture needs 30 minutes chilling to hold shape
Fry
Deep-fry at 175°C for 3-4 minutes until golden - double fry if needed for extra crisp
Serve
Immediate assembly with tahini, warm pita, pickles, and fresh vegetables

tl: Cover dried chickpeas with plenty of cold water, soak 12-24 hours until plump
Whisk tahini, water, lemon juice, garlic, salt until smooth and creamy
Dice tomato, slice cucumber, pick herb leaves, warm pita
Drain soaked chickpeas well, pulse with herbs, garlic, spices until coarse crumb
Refrigerate 30 minutes - this helps shape hold together
Heat vegetable oil to 175°C in deep pot
Form chickpea mixture into walnut-sized balls or patties
Deep-fry 3-4 minutes until golden and crisp, drain on paper
Pile falafel on plates, drizzle tahini, serve with pita, hummus, fresh vegetables
steps: Soak the chickpeas
Place dried chickpeas in a large bowl and cover with plenty of cold water. Let soak 12-24 hours until plump and hydrated. Drain well before using.
Dried chickpeas need full hydration for proper texture - canned chickpeas make mushy falafel.
Make the tahini sauce
Whisk tahini with water, lemon juice, minced garlic, and salt until smooth and creamy. Set aside - it will thicken slightly as it sits.
Tahini thickens when liquid is added, creating a creamy dipping sauce.
Prepare the falafel mixture

Pulse drained chickpeas in a food processor with parsley, coriander, garlic, cumin, ground coriander, baking powder, and salt until finely chopped but not pureed - the texture should be coarse crumbs that hold together when squeezed.
Overprocessing creates paste - you want texture that binds, not hummus.
Chill the mixture
Transfer falafel mixture to a bowl and refrigerate for 30 minutes. This step helps the mixture hold its shape when frying.
Chilling firms the mixture so balls don't fall apart in hot oil.
Heat the frying oil
Pour 5-7cm vegetable oil into a deep pot or wok and heat to 175°C. The oil is ready when a cube of bread browns in 15 seconds.
Proper temperature ensures crispy exterior without absorbing excess oil.
Shape the falafel
Scoop tablespoons of chilled mixture and form into walnut-sized balls or small patties. Wet your hands slightly to prevent sticking.
Uniform size ensures even cooking - balls or patties both work.
Fry until golden
Lower falafel into hot oil in batches and fry 3-4 minutes, turning occasionally, until deep golden brown and crisp. Drain on paper towels.
Frying time develops the signature crispy crust while cooking through.
Serve immediately
Arrange falafel on plates, drizzle generously with tahini sauce, and serve with warm pita, hummus, pickled vegetables, diced tomato, cucumber, and fresh herbs.
Falafel is best enjoyed hot and crisp from the fryer.

blurb: Greek summer classic - vegetables stuffed with rice, herbs, and tomatoes, baked until tender.

prov.famous_for: The Greek summer vegetable casserole - stuffed, baked, served cold or warm
prov.consists_of: Hollowed vegetables stuffed with rice-herb filling, baked in tomato sauce
prov.why_loved: Tender vegetables, flavourful rice filling, fresh herbs, versatile temperature
prov.method_detail: Oven - vegetables hollowed, stuffed with rice-herb mixture, baked in tomato sauce

brief.lead: Gemista captures the essence of Greek summer cooking - sweet capsicums and tender aubergine roasted until melting, stuffed with a rice filling fragrant with dill, parsley, and tomatoes. The vegetables steam and roast simultaneously, creating layers of flavour. Excellent warm or at room temperature, making it perfect for entertaining or summer meals.
expect: Prep veg
Hollow vegetables carefully, keeping tops as lids - chop flesh for the filling
Make filling
Raw rice cooks inside the vegetables as they roast - do not par-cook
Stuff
Pack filling loosely - rice expands as it cooks
Roast
Bake covered then uncovered for tender, slightly char-topped vegetables

tl: Slice capsicum tops, hollow aubergines lengthwise, scoop tomato flesh
Mix raw rice, chopped onions, herbs, olive oil, grated tomato, salt, pepper
Fill vegetables loosely with rice mixture, replace capsicum lids
Arrange stuffed vegetables in baking dish, tight packing helps steam
Pour passata mixed with olive oil and sugar around vegetables
Cover with foil, bake 45 minutes until vegetables tender
Remove foil, bake 15 minutes for slightly golden tops
Let rest 10 minutes before serving - helps flavours settle
steps: Prepare the vegetables

Slice the tops off capsicums and reserve as lids. Scoop out seeds and white membrane. Cut aubergines in half lengthwise and scoop out flesh, leaving 1cm shells. Grate or chop tomato flesh from 4 tomatoes, keeping the hollowed shells.
Hollowed vegetables become vessels for the rice filling as it steams and roasts.
Make the rice filling
Combine raw rice, finely chopped onions, spring onions, parsley, dill, olive oil, grated tomato flesh, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Mix well - the raw rice will cook inside the vegetables.
Raw rice expands as it roasts - this is the traditional Greek method.
Stuff the vegetables
Fill capsicums and aubergine shells generously with rice mixture, packing loosely to allow for expansion. Place capsicum lids back on top. Fill hollowed tomato shells with filling.
Loose packing prevents bursting as rice expands during cooking.
Arrange for baking

Place all stuffed vegetables in a large baking dish, packing them tightly. This helps create steam and keeps vegetables moist. In a small bowl, mix passata with olive oil and sugar, then pour around the vegetables.
Tight arrangement supports vegetables and creates self-basting steam.
Bake covered
Cover baking dish tightly with foil. Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until vegetables are tender when pierced with a knife and rice is cooked through.
Steaming under foil cooks rice and tenderises vegetables evenly.
Brown the tops
Remove foil and bake for another 15 minutes until the tops are slightly golden and the sauce has thickened around the vegetables.
Uncovered baking develops flavour and slight caramelisation.
Rest before serving
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes out of the oven. This allows the filling to set slightly and flavours to settle.
Resting time makes serving easier and flavours more cohesive.
Serve with feta
Transfer vegetables to plates, spoon over some of the tomato sauce from the dish, and crumble feta over the top. Serve with crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
Gemista is excellent warm, at room temperature, or cold - perfect for summer.

blurb: Mexican street classic - flour tortillas filled with cheese and folded until melty.

prov.popular_in: Mexico; worldwide as quick meal or snack
prov.famous_for: The ultimate quick Mexican comfort - melty cheese in toasted tortilla
prov.consists_of: Flour tortillas filled with cheese, folded, cooked until golden and melted
prov.name_origin: Mexican quesadilla (little cheesy thing); from queso (cheese)
prov.why_loved: Crispy toasted tortilla, melty cheese, endless filling variations
prov.method_detail: Stovetop - tortillas filled with cheese, folded, cooked until golden and melted

brief.lead: The perfect quick meal - quesadillas showcase the Mexican genius for transforming humble ingredients into something irresistible. Flour tortillas folded over melting cheese, griddled until crisp and golden. The classic version is just cheese, but add leftover chicken, sautéed vegetables, or beans for a complete meal. Serve with salsa, guacamole, and coriander for the full street-food experience.
expect: Heat pan
Medium-high heat creates crisp tortilla without burning
Build
Layer cheese and fillings on one half, fold over
Griddle
Cook 2-3 minutes per side until golden and cheese melts
Serve
Cut into wedges, serve immediately with salsas and guacamole

tl: Heat large pan over medium-high heat until hot
Add butter or oil to pan, swirl to coat
Place tortilla in pan, add cheese and fillings to one half, fold over
Cook 2-3 minutes until golden underneath and cheese starts melting
Flip quesadilla, cook second side 2-3 minutes until golden and crisp
Repeat with remaining tortillas and cheese
Cut each quesadilla into wedges, serve immediately with salsas, guacamole, lime
steps: Heat the pan
Place a large heavy-based pan over medium-high heat and let it get hot - 2-3 minutes. The pan needs to be hot enough to crisp the tortilla quickly.
Hot pan creates crisp tortilla while cheese melts.
Add fat to pan
Add butter or oil to the hot pan and swirl to coat the base. This prevents sticking and helps the tortilla crisp and colour.
Fat adds flavour and creates golden crispy tortilla.
Build the quesadilla

Place one tortilla in the hot pan. Sprinkle grated cheese over one half of the tortilla. Add any optional fillings (chicken, peppers, beans) evenly over the cheese. Fold the empty half over the filling to create a half-moon shape.
Folding creates even cooking and easy handling.
Cook first side
Cook the quesadilla for 2-3 minutes on the first side until the tortilla is golden brown and crisp underneath, and you can see the cheese beginning to melt.
First side crisps and starts the melting process.
Flip and cook second side
Carefully flip the quesadilla with a spatula. Cook the second side for 2-3 minutes until golden brown and crisp, and the cheese is fully melted and gooey.
Second side completes the crisping and melts cheese fully.
Repeat and serve
Repeat with remaining tortillas and cheese, adding more butter or oil to the pan as needed. Transfer cooked quesadillas to a board and cut each into 3-4 wedges.
Cutting into wedges makes them easy to dip and share.
Serve with accompaniments
Arrange quesadilla wedges on plates and serve immediately with salsa, guacamole, fresh coriander, and lime wedges for squeezing over.
Quesadillas are best eaten hot and crisp from the pan.
